references.title: A Robust Machine Learning Technique to Predict Low-performing Students

extracted.Courses:
Train: 5 Undergraduate Courses across 2 American Universities

Test: Following edition of the same  courses
extracted.Features: [Sequence of Question Responses on Clickers in the First 3 Weeks (Group and Individual)]
extracted.Moment of Prediction: 3rd week (out of 10 or 12 weeks depending on course)
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Clicker responses are collected and placed as a vector for each student.

Students who did not answer in the first 3 weeks were also removed.
extracted.Comments:
Authors create multiple course-specific models and start with a multi-institution analysis - comparing courses from similar institutions across different contexts. 

Hyper-parameter tuning is done using 10-Fold CV. 

Authors evaluate the same course across institutions to see differences and individually.
extracted.Limitations:
• Only one model is tested
  • No course-agnostic approach

annotations.text: Automated early prediction is important in large courses. Thus, authors propose a model that, using data from the first 3 weeks of a course, predict final exam scores - SVM. Data is collected on the first term and the model is deployed on the second term.

Modelling approach was used in 5 different Computer Science courses across 2 different institutions and taught by 3 different instructors. Predictive performance is competitive with state of the art techniques while having some strenghts:
